title: LDAP connections should be authenticated
description: When configured to accept the Anonymous or Unauthenticated authentication mechanism, an LDAP server will accept connections from clients that do not provide a password or other authentication credentials. Such users will be able to read or modify part or all of the data contained in the hosted directory. What is the potential impact? An attacker exploiting unauthenticated access to an LDAP server can access the data that is stored in the corresponding directory.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

never_trigger_on: Connections with Secure, Encryption, SecureSocketsLayer, Sealing, or Signing authentication
logic.reasoning: Detects DirectoryEntry instantiation without credentials or with Anonymous/None authentication. LDAP connections should use authenticated access with secure authentication types. Uses custom function for proper method-level test exclusion.

should_not_detect: var entry = new DirectoryEntry("LDAP://myserver", username, password);
